saudi_arabia_crude: Day 39: OPEC+ Apr 5 approves symbolic 206k bpd May quota increase but no real barrels; Apr exports slashed again — Yanbu-only Arab Light to Asia (2nd consecutive month); ~40M bbl to China (down from 48M Feb), ~23M bbl to India (down from 25-28M Feb); Mar exports collapsed to 4.355M bpd from 7.1M bpd in Feb; production target 10.2M bpd; Brent ~$109; Trump 48-hr ultimatum — Apr 6 power plant deadline TOMORROW — OilPrice, IndexBox, Bloomberg, CNBC, AGBI.
saudi_arabia_lng: Saudi Arabia has no meaningful LNG export capacity; Jafurah unconventional gas Phase 1 (450 mmscf/d) in early ramp; conflict underscores strategic urgency to diversify beyond crude — Rystad Energy.
saudi_arabia_lpg: Saudi LPG exports curtailed by reduced NGL throughput from offshore field closures; Yanbu bypass partially offsets but pipeline bottleneck (~4.5M bpd port capacity) constrains total volumes — Kpler.
uae_crude: Day 39: BOROUGE SUSPENDED Apr 5 — 3 fires from air defense debris (Bloomberg); OPEC+ kept targets unchanged (Jun 7 next); ADCOP pipeline at 1.62M bpd; Fujairah exports holding; UAE cumulative: 507 BMs + 2,191 UAVs + 24 CMs intercepted; Habshan gas shut again Apr 3 (1 killed); >50% crude offline; Trump 'Power Plant Day' Apr 6 TOMORROW — Bloomberg, Gulf News, S&P Global, Alma Center.
uae_lng: Das Island LNG (6 Mtpa) at minimal throughput; Habshan SHUT AGAIN Apr 3 — falling debris from intercepted Iranian munition sparked fire at world's largest gas processing facility (6.1 Bcf/d); Shah Gas (~1 Bcf/d) still offline since Mar 17; ADNOC Gas adjusting production on transaction-by-transaction basis; Ruwais LNG (9.6 Mtpa) under construction, ops 2028 — Bloomberg, MEED, Energy Connects.
uae_lpg: UAE LPG exports severely disrupted: Shah Gas Field still shut (~1 Bcf/d), Ruwais Refinery-2 offline (major NGL source); Habshan SHUT AGAIN Apr 3 — NGL processing further curtailed; exports constrained by Hormuz closure — Bloomberg, Kpler, OilPrice.
iraq_crude: Day 39: Iran declared Iraqi ships EXEMPT from Hormuz restrictions (Apr 4); Kirkuk-Ceyhan at 250k bpd (contract expires Jul 2026); KRG deal adds potential 200k bpd more; SOMO Syria route operational — fuel oil exports through Baniyas began Apr 2 (50k bpd crude + 650k mt/month overland); total exports ~300-500k bpd vs pre-war 3.3M bpd; crude output collapsed to 1.4M bpd from 4.3M — The National, TRT World, Pipeline Technology Journal.
iraq_lng: Iraq has no LNG export infrastructure; associated gas production collapsed alongside 76% crude output reduction; Basra Gas Company projects stalled — Rystad Energy.
iraq_lpg: Iraq's LPG exports effectively halted following FM declaration and shutdown of Basra export terminals; Khor Mor gas processing in Kurdistan also halted — IEA Oil Market Report.
kuwait_crude: Day 39: KPC Shuwaikh HQ set ablaze by drones Apr 5 + 2 power/desal plants struck with significant material damage — 9+ major attacks total; production collapsed to ~500k bpd (81% from 2.59M); KPC FM on all exports; producing only for domestic consumption; OPEC+ quota increase academic as exports physically blocked; Al-Ahmadi refinery (346k bpd) hit again — Bloomberg, The National, Times Kuwait, Argus Media, Al Jazeera.
kuwait_lng: Kuwait's Al-Zour LNG import FSRU faces acute supply uncertainty as Qatar's 77 Mtpa entirely offline; Asian spot LNG past $25/MMBtu; Al-Zour at partial ops for domestic power — Argus Media.
kuwait_lpg: Kuwait's LPG exports disrupted after Al-Ahmadi refinery drone strikes; NGL extraction from multiple processing units shut; KPC 3-4 months to restore full capacity post-war — Kpler, Cyprus Mail.
iran_crude: Day 39: F-15E WSO RESCUED by US commandos + CIA after 24-hr evasion in Iranian mountains (2 US aircraft destroyed on ground during extraction); Trump 48-hr ultimatum — Apr 6 power plant deadline TOMORROW; Kharg Island loading ~1.5 mb/d with exports INCREASING despite war; Iran parliament energy chair confirms exports rose; Mar 13 US strikes hit 90+ military targets but spared oil infrastructure; Brent ~$109 — CBS News, NBC News, Axios, Washington Post, Al Arabiya, Kpler.
iran_lng: Iran has no LNG export capacity; Israeli strikes on South Pars Phases 3-6, 14, 17-18 took ~12% of gas output offline; domestic supply severely strained; IRGC threatening retaliatory strikes on Gulf desalination/energy infrastructure if power plants targeted — S&P Global Platts, NBC News.
iran_lpg: Iran's LPG exports face disruption from ~30% shut-in of southern NGL-linked oil fields; IRGC toll corridor charging ~$2M/vessel in yuan/crypto; first Western ships paying toll; Iran-Oman transit protocol being drafted; Philippine-flagged vessels granted access Apr 2 — Vortexa, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, NPR.
qatar_crude: All Qatar offshore oil fields (~440 kb/d) shut since early March; Laffan Refineries 1 and 2 offline; QatarEnergy FM encompasses all hydrocarbon exports; OPEC+ quota increase academic — Bloomberg, S&P Global.
qatar_lng: All 77 Mtpa halted since Mar 2; 2 of 14 trains destroyed (12.5 Mtpa, 3-5 yr rebuild); FM through mid-June minimum; BUT Golden Pass LNG (Texas, QE 70%/Exxon 30%) achieved first production Mar 30 — FERC approved commissioning cargoes Apr 1; feedgas surged to 434 mmcf/d Apr 2; first export cargo loading ~Apr 20 via HL Sea Eagle; Train 1 adds 6 Mtpa; 20% global LNG supply gap persists — PGJ Online, Bloomberg, Al Arabiya, Natural Gas Intelligence, Gulf Times.
qatar_lpg: Qatar's LPG exports (byproduct of LNG production at Ras Laffan) halted since Mar 2 along with all downstream operations; restart impossible until conflict ends — QatarEnergy statement.
oman_crude: Oman-Iran deputy FM-level talks Apr 5 on 'smooth transit' and joint Hormuz management protocol — could include permit system (Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye); 3 Omani-flagged vessels transited Apr 2; inland production ~950 kb/d intact; Oman benchmark $152-153/bbl — highest-priced crude globally; Mina Al Fahal exports uninterrupted — Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye, CNBC, Zawya, Hellenic Shipping News.
oman_lng: Oman LNG's 10.4 Mtpa facility (Qalhat complex, 3 trains) outside Hormuz chokepoint still running at full capacity; commanding massive Asian spot premiums with Qatar's 77 Mtpa offline; long-term deals with Mercuria (800k tpa), Vitol (800k tpa), Shell (1.6 Mtpa) all flowing — S&P Platts LNG Daily, Kpler.
oman_lpg: Oman's LPG exports from Fahud and Saih Rawl gas processing largely unaffected; inland production and non-Hormuz export position provide insulation from regional disruption — Kpler.
bahrain_crude: Day 39: GPIC petrochemical plant + Bapco fuel storage tank struck by drones Apr 5 — 13-drone wave repelled; 7th+ direct Iranian attack; Sitra refinery (405 kb/d) offline since early Mar; Bapco FM on all ops; Abu Safah (150k bpd Bahrain share) shut; OPEC+ no new barrels (Jun 7 next) — Gulf News, Xinhua, GDN Online, The National, Argus Media.
bahrain_lng: Bahrain depends on Saudi pipeline gas and LNG imports for energy security; Sitra refinery destruction eliminated domestic refining; regional gas disruption compounds risk — Gulf News.
bahrain_lpg: Bahrain's LPG exports ceased entirely following Sitra refinery destruction and Bapco Energies FM declaration — Argus Media.
russia_crude: US sanctions waiver (Mar 12-Apr 11) allows Russian crude delivery; India imports surged 90% MoM; Russia-to-China seaborne crude doubled YoY to 1.8 mb/d; new Asian buyers (Philippines, S. Korea, Vietnam, Thailand, Sri Lanka); shadow fleet at 640 vessels; G7+ tankers still carry 33% of exports; EU 18th sanctions package adds 43 vessels; Rosneft+Lukoil seaborne volumes down 83% YoY in sanctioned trade — OilPrice, GTR, Moscow Times, CREA, Brookings.
russia_lng: EU ban on Russian LNG short-term contracts takes effect April 25; Yamal LNG sent 100% of Feb exports to EU; Russia redirecting cargoes to Asia; Arctic LNG-2 below capacity; TotalEnergies may halt Yamal exports post-ban but stays in project; Asian spot surges ~39%; long-term contracts valid until Jan 2027 — Interfax, ICIS, S&P Global, Riviera Maritime.
russia_lpg: Russia bans gasoline exports from April 1 to secure domestic supply; LPG exports remain small share of global trade; Gulf conflict marginally improved Russian NGL competitiveness in Asian markets — Business Today, Argus Media.
us_crude: US crude exports surged to ~4.5 mb/d (near records) with buyer base expanding to 15 countries incl. first-time destinations; SPR 45.2 mb delivered of 172 mb authorized; IEA weighing 2nd coordinated release; Gulf Coast refiners enjoying strongest margin environment in years; EIA forecasts 13.6M bpd production in 2026 — BIC Magazine, CNBC, Fortune, EIA.
us_lng: Cheniere holds ~50% of US export capacity (11% global supply) with Qatar offline; all major US terminals at max capacity; Golden Pass LNG first production Mar 30, FERC approved commissioning cargoes Apr 1, feedgas at 434 mmcf/d, first cargo loading ~Apr 20; Plaquemines Phase 2 + Corpus Christi Stage III also online; US LNG exports forecast +15% to 16.4 Bcf/d in 2026 — Fortune, EIA, PGJ Online, BIC Magazine, Natural Gas Intelligence.
us_lpg: US Gulf Coast LPG exports from Enterprise and Targa terminals benefiting from sharply higher Asian demand as Saudi, UAE, Qatari NGL supply curtailed; LPG-naphtha spread favours increased US exports — Argus LPG World.
china_crude: Jan-Feb imports 11.99 mb/d (+15.8% YoY) — pre-conflict stockpiling; Gulf share fell from 51% to 44%; Russia share surging (seaborne doubled YoY to 1.8 mb/d); 13% from Iran via Kharg; Sinopec cut runs 10% (~500k bpd); China halted diesel/gasoline exports to protect domestic supply; teapots pivoting to Venezuelan and Iranian heavy grades for Q2 — Vortexa, OilPrice, Columbia CGEP, The Diplomat.
china_lng: QatarEnergy extended FM to Chinese LNG contracts Mar 24; Wanhua Chemical FM on Middle East supplies; Shell-CNOOC Huizhou ethylene cracker shut; spot LNG costs surged ~39%; pipeline gas via Power of Siberia provides partial insulation; EU Russian LNG ban (Apr 25) may redirect more Russian cargoes to Asia — Reuters, ICIS, Hydrocarbon Processing.
china_lpg: China (world's largest LPG importer) faces tightening supply as ME NGL-linked LPG from Saudi, UAE, Qatar offline; PDH plant operators scrambling for US and Australian cargoes at sharply higher prices — ICIS.
india_crude: Russian crude imports surged 90% MoM; Russia offered to 'steadily increase' crude + LNG supplies (Apr 3 bilateral Puri-Sorokin meeting); overall crude imports down ~15% in March; ~25 days crude/fuel stocks; Operation Urja Suraksha escorts continuing; MRPL declared FM on gasoline exports (Mar+Apr); government ordered refiners to divert propane/butane to cooking gas — Business Standard, Argus Media, ChemAnalyst, The Diplomat.
india_lng: Qatar supplied 41.4% of India's LNG — now plunged 92% in March; Petronet FM declared; Gujarat Gas imposed 50% industrial cuts; GAIL bought Oman LNG spot cargo; India negotiating Russian LNG purchases after Apr 3 meeting; 2026 LNG imports may reach 29M mt if supply secured — Business Standard, BusinessToday, S&P Global.
india_lpg: Indian Navy escorted LPG tankers Pine Gas and Jag Vasant (92,000t combined) through Hormuz under Operation Urja Suraksha; government ordered IOC/BPCL/HPCL to stop cracking propane/butane for petrochems and prioritize LPG cooking gas output; ONGC Opal Dahej plant impacted — Business Today, Argus Media.
japan_crude: Apr tanker arrivals set to plummet; 45 Japanese-entity ships stranded in Gulf; 93.5% crude ME-dependent (94.2% in Feb); crude imports fell to 3.5 mb/d in late March (lowest since 2016 in Vortexa dataset); first Japan-linked LNG tanker (MOL Sohar LNG) crossed Hormuz Apr 3 — milestone; Asia has limited options to diversify from Mideast reliance — S&P Global, gCaptain, Vortexa, Reuters.
japan_lng: JKM Asian LNG spot $20.39/MMBtu (Apr 1) — 2x pre-crisis; Daiwa warns 10% ME LNG cut pushes Japan GDP negative; Mitsubishi Chemical cut ethylene output at Kashima + Mizushima; naphtha surged past $1,000/mt; Japan relies on US spot + Australian contracts as backstop — CME Group, Daiwa Institute, C&EN.
japan_lpg: Japan's LPG imports for petrochemical and residential heating face tightened supply and surging prices; naphtha-fed crackers cutting rates as feedstock costs exceed finished chemical values; diversification to US/Australian cargoes at significant premium — Argus Media, C&EN.
south_korea_crude: Government elevated crude crisis alert to 'Caution'; SPR release of 22.46M bbl (record); inventories up ~13% MoM; Lotte Yeosu halted; naphtha export ban since Mar 27; naphtha designated 'economic security' item; YNCC declared FM + mothballing No.3 aromatics complex; LG Chem shut Yeosu No.2 plant (1.2 Mtpa ethylene), cut cracker rates to 60% — S&P Global, ICIS, Korea Times, Seoul Economic Daily.
south_korea_lng: KOGAS reports inventory exceeds mandatory reserves; Qatar share only ~14% of 2026 imports; Woodside 0.5 Mtpa deal (10.5yr) commenced 2026; TotalEnergies 1 Mtpa deal from end-2027; supply concerns downplayed but JKM at $20+/MMBtu raises costs — Cyprus Mail, KOGAS, S&P Global.
south_korea_lpg: South Korea's LPG imports for naphtha-alternative crackers disrupted by ME supply shutdown; LPG-naphtha price spread shifted as both feedstocks face Gulf-linked constraints; naphtha designated as 'economic security' item, exports restricted — ICIS, Korea Times.
taiwan_crude: Formosa Petrochemical (FPCC) FM on petrochemical supplies incl. ethylene and propylene; Formosa Chemicals & Fibre FM from April on styrene/phenol/PTA; Formosa Plastics Corp FM on PE downstream effective Apr 1; FPCC crackers #2 and #3 at ~70%, may shut one if naphtha runs out; Costco imposing plastic packaging purchase limits — Baird Maritime, DigiTimes, Hydrocarbon Processing, Taiwan News, Packaging Insights.
taiwan_lng: Taiwan's LNG import dependency tested as ME-origin naphtha disrupted; crackers prioritizing limited feedstock for ethylene over LPG derivatives; no direct LNG FM reported but downstream plastics crisis deepening — DigiTimes, Hydrocarbon Processing.
taiwan_lpg: Taiwan LPG supply constrained as ME NGL shutdowns and Hormuz closure cut propane/butane availability; Formosa Group FM declarations encompass NGL-derivative products — Taiwan News, ICIS.
thailand_crude: Thailand secured bilateral Hormuz transit deal with Iran (Mar 28); Deputy PM confirmed talks opening on Russian crude purchases; SE Asia shutting offices and imposing travel limits as oil crisis deepens; Rayong Olefins declared FM; Vietnam suspended fuel exports; ASEAN ministers warn of inflationary pressures — Al Jazeera, Moscow Times, C&EN, Borneo Post, NPR.
thailand_lng: Thailand's growing LNG import dependency (declining domestic gas from Gulf of Thailand/Myanmar pipeline) tested as spot LNG surged well beyond pre-crisis levels; limited storage buffer; ASEAN ministers flagged LNG supply risk — S&P Global, The Diplomat, Borneo Post.
thailand_lpg: Thailand's small LPG imports for petrochemical feedstock and cooking fuel face higher prices; Iran bilateral deal may ease some transit but underlying ME supply still severely constrained — Argus Media, Borneo Post.
vietnam_crude: Nghi Son Refinery turning to US and alternative crudes as Kuwait supply (~80% of imports) stalls; Decree 72/2026 cut MFN tariffs on petrol/diesel/naphtha to 0% (Mar 9-Apr 30); PM Chinh called Kuwaiti PM; asked Japan/S.Korea for help accessing crude; 85% Gulf-dependent — most vulnerable SE Asian importer; Vietnam suspended fuel exports — Argus Media, The Diplomat, Nation Thailand, NPR.
vietnam_lng: First 2026 LNG cargo of 63,000t received at Thi Vai terminal late Feb; PV GAS confirms supply through April; second FSRU at Vung Tau faces headwinds from surging spot prices — Vietnam.vn, PV GAS.
vietnam_lpg: Vietnam's LPG imports (~80% Middle East propane-dependent) face higher procurement costs; relatively small volumes limit absolute exposure — ICIS.
opec_plus: Apr 5 JMMC: approved symbolic 206k bpd May quota increase 'in principle' but no real barrels — acknowledges damaged infrastructure cannot restart; Apr 206k increase (approved Mar 1) physically undeliverable for Kuwait/Bahrain/Qatar; JMMC warned attacks on infrastructure 'undermining market stability'; Iraq/Iran exempt from Hormuz but limited alternatives; next meeting Jun 7; war dictating actual supply regardless of quotas — The National, CNBC, Bloomberg, Middle East Insider, OPEC.
petrochemical_crisis: Global naphtha surged past $1,000/mt (from $776 Mar 6, now $1,010-1,050 C&F Japan); 20-25M mt ethylene production expected lost (~12% of 2025 global output); FM cascade: FPCC + Formosa Group (Taiwan), YNCC + LG Chem (S. Korea), Aster Chemicals (Singapore), Chandra Asri (Indonesia), Rayong Olefins (Thailand), LyondellBasell (Europe — polymer lines FM), MRPL (India), Wanhua Chemical (China); 84% of ME polyethylene exports dependent on Hormuz; US-Asia polymer arbitrage widened to $1,200/mt; PE prices surged 40%+ in 2 weeks — C&EN, ChemAnalyst, Hydrocarbon Processing, Chemical Week, Polymer Update.
hormuz_status: Day 39: Commercial vessel crossings down 90-95% since Feb 28; IRGC toll corridor charging ~$2M/vessel in yuan/crypto; first Western ships paying toll; Iran-Oman transit protocol being drafted; Iran passed bill formalizing Hormuz toll system; friendly-nation exemptions: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand, Philippines; 20+ vessels used new corridor; Omani-flagged vessels transiting via alternative coastal route; Trump 48-hr ultimatum expires Apr 6 — Wikipedia, CNBC, CNN, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, Lloyd's List, NPR, Washington Times.
